notes: STRUCTURAL gates graded by skill-creator's grader. hf-voiceover is NOT a visual skill — it produces audio + a timing JSON, so there are no [media-judge] expectations; the grader checks files on disk, the JSON shape, and fidelity to the script. Assumes a valid 02-script.md from hf-script and a reachable ai-gen audio proxy (keyless). Derived from 1-requirements.md JTBD-1 (voiceover+timing row: 'word-level timestamps present' [structural]) and JTBD-4 (re-voice, facts preserved). The TTS-unreachable case exercises the documented silent fallback (never fail / never prompt).

evals.name: voiceover-and-word-timing
evals.prompt: Generate the voiceover and word-level timing for the api-rate-limit-teaser from its 02-script.md.
expectations: writes ONE non-empty continuous track artifacts/api-rate-limit-teaser/assets/vo/narration.wav via a single ai-gen TTS call (no per-beat wavs are produced by default)
writes artifacts/api-rate-limit-teaser/04-timing.json with a non-empty top-level words[] track, each entry having text/start/end (and a beat tag)

04-timing.json has a beats[] array, one entry per script beat (split from the single transcript by order), each with beat/wav/duration/timing_method and a words[] (timing_method is wizper when per-word timestamps were returned, else even); each beat's wav points at assets/vo/narration.wav
the narration text is exactly the VO lines from 02-script.md, joined in order — no sentences invented, dropped, or paraphrased
the flat words[] track is in absolute timeline time (word starts increase monotonically across beats; total_duration covers the whole narration)
the reply states the resolved voice (e.g. am_michael) and model (fal-ai/kokoro/american-english) and notes whether word timings are real (wizper) or even/estimated; state.md phase 4 is marked done
no HeyGen auth/TTS/ASR is used; the run is keyless via ai-gen (one TTS + one ASR call) and does not prompt the user

evals.name: re-voice-different-voice-facts-preserved
evals.prompt: Re-voice the api-rate-limit-teaser with the af_nova voice.
expectations: re-reads the UNCHANGED 02-script.md (does not call hf-script; narration text is identical to the prior version)
re-generates assets/vo/narration.wav and 04-timing.json using voice af_nova; the word strings in 04-timing.json are the same as before (only timings/audio differ)
appends a dated note to state.md recording the re-voice (am_michael -> af_nova) and that the script facts were unchanged
the reply reports the new voice af_nova was used

evals.name: tts-unreachable-silent-fallback
evals.prompt: Generate the voiceover for the offline-demo project (ai-gen TTS is unreachable in this environment).
expectations: does NOT fail the project and does NOT prompt the user when ai-gen TTS returns no wav / success:false

still writes artifacts/offline-demo/04-timing.json with one beat entry per script beat using script-estimated pacing (timing_method estimated, empty words[]), so beat pacing is still available downstream
records the silent fallback in state.md (which beats fell back and why) so hf-render knows there is no audio stream to mux
the reply honestly reports that the voiceover fell back to silent because ai-gen TTS was unreachable
